title: RISK: Hardcoded mainnet lightwalletd endpoints with no health monitoring
description:
Three hardcoded endpoints (ECC, zec.rocks, Free2z) have no automated health checks or visibility into uptime. A solo maintainer only learns of failures when users report issues. Community-operated endpoints (zec.rocks, zuul.free2z.cash) have no SLAs. When an operator shuts down, the only remedy is a code change + release. Round-robin failover provides no preference for known-good endpoints and creates latency on every failure. 

Long-term mitigation strategies to consider (not in scope for this task):
- Periodic health check endpoint that reports status
- Configuration file for endpoint updates without code release
- Graceful deprecation mechanism that warns users before removing an endpoint
- Monitoring integration (e.g., pagerduty alert if all endpoints fail)
close_reason: Known risk documented; long-term mitigations noted for future roadmap
